cards.title: Indemnity vs Fixed Benefit
cards.content:
ΓÇó Indemnity (Mediclaim): Pays the actual hospital bill up to the Sum Insured.
ΓÇó Fixed Benefit (Critical Illness / Daily Cash): Pays a predetermined lump sum upon diagnosis, regardless of actual expenses.

cards.title: Top-up & Super Top-up
cards.content:
ΓÇó Top-up: Kicks in only when a SINGLE claim exceeds the deductible amount.
ΓÇó Super Top-up: Kicks in when the AGGREGATE of all claims in a year exceeds the deductible. (Much better for chronic patients).

items.text: One single Sum Insured shared by the entire family. Cost-effective.
Ayushman Bharat: Provides Γé╣5 Lakhs per family per year for secondary/tertiary care to vulnerable families.
Overseas Travel covers medical emergencies abroad. Personal Accident covers Death/Disability.

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: Co-pay: A fixed percentage (e.g., 10%) of the claim amount that the insured must pay from their own pocket.
Deductible: A fixed amount (e.g., Γé╣50,000) that must be borne by the insured before the policy starts paying.
Sub-limits: Maximum limits placed on specific treatments (e.g., Cataract limit Γé╣30,000) or Room Rent (e.g., 1% of Sum Insured).
Critical Illness covers 36 specified major diseases (like Cancer, Stroke) and pays a lump sum on survival of 30 days post-diagnosis.
Micro Insurance policies are designed for economically weaker sections with affordable premiums.
